codeReview.summary: The package/layout implementation is largely aligned with the feature scope, but VAL-TUI-003 and VAL-TUI-004 are not adequately validated by the added tests, leaving required behavior unproven and regression-prone.

issues.description: VAL-TUI-003 requires asserting `papierklammer-tui --help` usage content (including `--url` and `--api-key`), but this test file only checks package.json metadata and never exercises/help-verifies the CLI output.

VAL-TUI-004 requires proving Ctrl+C triggers graceful exit/terminal restoration, but this test does not assert exit behavior (it manually unmounts after writing Ctrl+C). It can pass even if Ctrl+C handling regresses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS scope is stricter than practical implementation needs for new workspace packages.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md line 14 restricts changes to `packages/orchestrator-tui/` and `.factory/`, but this feature necessarily changed root `vitest.config.ts` (project registration) and `pnpm-lock.yaml` (dependency resolution).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure compliance reporting appears inconsistent with transcript order.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tui-worker SKILL.md line 31 says 'Write tests first', while transcript skeleton shows implementation files were created before tests; handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail: implementation is close to scope, but two blocking validation gaps remain—CLI help behavior and Ctrl+C graceful-exit behavior are not asserted in tests per VAL-TUI-003/004.
